changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "It Hasn't Been a While"

TextENGNew: ``...I keep telling you, this isn’t our first meeting! We’ve been seeing each other every day until yesterday, haven’t we?``
changeReason: Old translates 初めまして as 'it's been a while'; new translates as 'this isn't our first meeting' — 初めまして means 'nice to meet you (for the first time),' not 'it's been a while.' The title and context indicate Shion is correcting someone who keeps treating their meeting as a first meeting.

TextENGNew: ``...Forget it. If you say so, then fine.``
Someone from Shion’s household came to pick us up in a station wagon.
changeReason: Old says 'came to get me'; new says 'came to pick us up' — 'me' vs 'us' is a meaningful difference in who was picked up.

TextENGNew: I’d initially refused, but Shion insisted, and before I knew it, both my bicycle and I had been loaded into the car.
The car was currently making its way down the bumpy road to Hinamizawa.
Shion was... apparently as skilled an actress as Mion, if not more so—no matter how I pressed her, she’d dodge and weave out of every question.
``Still... you two really do look alike. If you tied your hair back like Mion, would you be completely identical?``
``Who knows? Probably.@k@vS20/06/440600003. We’re genuine identical twins, after all.@k@vS20/06/440600004. When we were little, all we had to do was swap our clothes and nobody could tell us apart.``
``We used to switch places at every opportunity and fool all sorts of people. Ahahaha!``
The middle-aged driver in a black suit—looking every bit the part of a butler—let out a deep sigh.
``What’s the matter, Kasai? That was quite a deep sigh.``
``My apologies. ...I was merely thinking that some things never change.``
Through the rearview mirror, I could see years upon years of accumulated hardship written on the driver’s face.

``Anyway, Kei-chan, is your house this way? Kasai doesn’t know Hinamizawa beyond the road to the Sonozaki main house.@k@vS20/06/440600008. If you leave it to him, he’ll drive you all the way to Yagouchi.``
``I-I’d rather not! ...Excuse me, could you stop at the narrow road on the next right?@k@vS20/01/440100087. That’ll be fine.``
The car stopped at the requested spot.
The driver—addressed as Kasai—unloaded my bicycle from the back.
``Ah... thank you very much. Thanks for the ride today...``
``Keiichi-san, was it? Your name.``
``Huh? Oh, yes.``
``...I imagine there will be various tribulations ahead, but I believe she’ll lose interest eventually. Please bear with it until then.``
A look of deep, profound sympathy.
...This man had undoubtedly been dealing with the Sonozaki sisters’ antics since they were small children.
``However, like Mion-san, she’s a kind person at heart.``
``...Meaning she’ll be just as much trouble as Mion?``
The man’s smile froze, and he was at a loss for words.@k...Hey, at least defend her a little.
changeReason: Old says 'Hey, back me up here' (asking for support); new says 'Hey, at least defend her a little' (asking to defend Shion) — different request: defending the speaker vs defending Shion, per Japanese 少しはフォローしろ (give some follow-up/backing).

TextENGNew: ``Well, bye, Kei-chan! Let’s see each other again soon. Give my regards to my sister!@k@vS20/06/440600010. Maybe I’ll start attending the Hinamizawa school~``
``Don’t you dare. If you transfer here, I’ll transfer out to Okinomiya.``
``Wow, that’s really cruel, Kei-chan...!``
A short beep of the horn.@kThe man gave a small wave from the driver’s seat.
The car disappeared down the dark night road, leaving a cloud of dust in its wake...
...Honestly... what an utterly bewildering day.
...Mion’s face when she stumbled upon me with Shion—her expression like a pigeon hit by a pellet gun—had belatedly made quite the impression.
